title: START_STICKY orphans service after process kill
description: Android kills the Flutter process under memory pressure. START_STICKY causes NieForegroundService to restart with a null intent (handled as ACTION_START), showing the 'nie is running' notification — but no Flutter engine, no WebSocket, no relay connection. The Dart _running flag is false in the new process so BackgroundService.stop() can never be called until the user manually opens the app. Users will file issues: 'notification says running but I\'m not using nie'. Fix: use START_NOT_STICKY. A relay-connectivity service should only run when the user is actively connected, not auto-resurrect.
